preamble: The spirantization of a voiceless retroflex stop [ʈ] into a voiceless retroflex fricative [ʂ]. This shift is part of a lenition chain often mediated by an intermediate affricate stage.

examples.note: Documented historical shift in some Atlantic varieties.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
